body.text: **Top story:** Claude surges to #1 on the App Store as the #QuitGPT boycott hits 2.5 million users following OpenAI's Pentagon deal — while the Pro-Human AI Declaration reaches mainstream coverage and Google ships a Workspace CLI with native MCP server support.

items.text: **📱 1. #QuitGPT Peaks: Claude Hits #1 on App Store, 2.5M Boycotters**

body.text:
The consumer backlash against OpenAI's Pentagon deal reached extraordinary scale by March 7:

• **2.5 million** people cancelled subscriptions or publicly pledged to boycott ChatGPT
• ChatGPT U.S. uninstalls: **+295% day-over-day**
• One-star reviews: **+775% surge**
• Claude: jumped from **#42 → #1** on the U.S. App Store, directly displacing ChatGPT

Inside OpenAI: Caitlin Kalinowski (hardware & robotics lead since Nov 2024) resigned over the deal; 30+ OpenAI employees signed public statements supporting Anthropic's legal challenge. The DOD signed its OpenAI deal within 24 hours of designating Anthropic a supply-chain risk — a sequence multiple OpenAI employees protested internally.

This is the first consumer-scale AI boycott in the industry's history, producing a measurable, rapid market share swing. AI brand trust is now a competitive variable.

items.text: **🗺️ 2. Pro-Human AI Declaration: The Roadmap Nobody Agreed On**

body.text:
TechCrunch published "A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen" (March 7), bringing mainstream attention to the Pro-Human AI Declaration.

**Key demands:**
• Development of superintelligence should be **prohibited** until broad scientific consensus + public buy-in
• All powerful AI systems must have **kill-switch mechanisms** for human operators
• AI must not self-replicate, autonomously self-improve, resist shutdown, or control WMDs

**The unusual coalition:** Steve Bannon, Glenn Beck, Richard Branson, Ralph Nader, Susan Rice, Nobel economist Daron Acemoglu — plus Future of Life Institute, Pause AI, MIRI, and 40+ organizations.

**The polling:** 95% of Americans oppose an unregulated race to superintelligence.

**The gap:** The TechCrunch critique notes signatories represent almost no one with actual control over frontier AI development, and the prohibitions run directly counter to current U.S. administration policy.

items.text: **🔧 3. Google Ships Workspace CLI with Built-In MCP Server**

body.text:
Google released `gws` — a CLI for the entire Google Workspace API surface — designed for both developers and AI agents:

• Covers Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Chat, Admin, and all other Workspace APIs
• Written in Rust, distributed via npm; Apache-2.0 open source
• **Built-in MCP server** exposing all Workspace APIs to any MCP-compatible AI agent over stdio — no custom connectors needed
• Dynamically built from Google's Discovery Service: auto-updates when Google adds new API endpoints
• 40+ agent skills; 100+ total skills including higher-level workflows; 50+ curated recipes

Direct threat to Zapier and Make for AI agent use cases. Developers no longer need third-party connectors to build agentic Workspace integrations.

items.text: **🏢 4. Oracle Plans 20,000–30,000 Layoffs for AI Infrastructure Pivot**

body.text:
Oracle is cutting 20,000–30,000 employees — generating $8–10B in capital — specifically to fund AI infrastructure investment. Layoffs begin this month.

• Cuts span multiple divisions; AI infrastructure is the stated reallocation target
• Follows Block's announced 4,000-person cut (~40% of staff), also explicitly citing AI efficiency
• Together: the two largest explicitly AI-motivated workforce reductions in tech history within the same week

The pattern: boards under mounting pressure to show AI ROI in concrete financial terms. Workforce restructuring is becoming the visible output of that pressure.

items.text: **⚖️ 5. Anthropic Pentagon: Designation Confirmed, Lawsuit Days Away**

body.text:
Anthropic officially confirmed the supply-chain risk designation — a label historically reserved for foreign adversaries like Huawei.

**Legal strategy being finalized:**
• **First Amendment** — designation punishes Anthropic for publishing AI safety positions (no mass surveillance, no autonomous weapons)
• **Fifth Amendment** — severe penalties imposed without due process or hearing
• **APA** — Pentagon acted arbitrarily and without statutory authority

**Filing targets:** U.S. District Court (N.D. California) and federal appeals court in Washington, D.C.

**Industry support:** Tech industry groups preparing amicus briefs; State and Treasury reportedly reviewing Claude usage; 30+ OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees filed public statements of support.

*(Formal lawsuits filed March 9; TRO hearing set for March 24.)*

• **DeepSeek V4** — Day 8+ with no launch. Possible "V4 Lite" update spotted on DeepSeek's website (expanded context handling) but unconfirmed. Community pressure escalating as GPT-5.4 benchmark narrative solidifies.
• **Samsung** — Reconfirmed plans to double Gemini AI-equipped devices from ~400M to **800 million** by end of 2026 at MWC wrap-up events
• **MWC 2026 concluded** — Barcelona (March 3–6); AI glasses from Alibaba/Qwen, Samsung, and others dominated the hardware showcase
• **Florida AI bill** — Gov. DeSantis's comprehensive AI regulation bill advancing; final vote expected this week

• **#QuitGPT durability** — Whether the App Store surge translates to sustained Claude retention or normalizes within days
• **Anthropic lawsuit filing** — Expected March 9–10; emergency TRO request will determine whether the designation is paused during litigation
• **DeepSeek V4** — Every passing day strengthens GPT-5.4's benchmark narrative; an eventual drop could dramatically shift the competitive picture
• **Oracle layoff reaction** — Whether AI-justified workforce cuts face meaningful regulatory or customer pushback
